items.definition_en: Inventory is the total amount of goods a store has for sale.
items.example_en: We need to check the inventory before reordering products.

items.definition_en: Checkout is the place in a shop where you pay for your items.
items.example_en: There is a long line at the checkout today.

items.definition_en: A supply chain is the system of people and activities involved in delivering products to stores.
items.example_en: The supply chain was disrupted by the recent storm.

items.definition_en: Merchandise is the goods that are bought and sold in shops.
items.example_en: The new merchandise arrived early this morning.

items.definition_en: Market share is the percentage of sales a company holds compared to competitors.
items.example_en: They are working hard to increase their market share.

items.definition_en: A franchise is a store using the brand and system of an established company.
items.example_en: He owns a large fast-food franchise in the city.

items.definition_en: A retailer is a person or company that sells goods to the public.
items.example_en: The retailer offers discounts to loyal customers.

items.definition_en: Sales volume is the total number of products sold in a period.
items.example_en: Their sales volume increased by ten percent last year.

items.definition_en: A purchase order is a document sent to a supplier to request products.
items.example_en: The manager submitted a purchase order for more laptops.

items.definition_en: Self-service allows customers to choose and pay for products without help.
items.example_en: The store introduced a self-service checkout option last month.

items.definition_en: A wholesaler is a company that sells goods in large quantities to retailers.
items.example_en: The retailer ordered all their goods from a local wholesaler.

items.definition_en: A point of sale is the time and place where a sale happens.
items.example_en: They updated their point of sale system last week.

items.definition_en: A display unit is a structure used to show products to customers.
items.example_en: The store uses a display unit for new arrivals.

items.definition_en: A stockroom is a storage area for goods in a store.
items.example_en: The stockroom is full of unsold items.

items.definition_en: Turnover is the amount of money a business earns from sales in a period.
items.example_en: The company's turnover doubled compared to the previous year.

items.definition_en: A warehouse is a large storage area for goods.
items.example_en: The warehouse is located in the industrial park.
